title: typeof variable — type query on a runtime value
description: `typeof config` where `config` is a bound value resolves to the value's static type. Without `as const` the type is widened (`url: string`, `port: number`); with `as const` it pins to literals. This case verifies the widened path.
notes: `typeof <variable>` reads the declared / inferred type of a value. Validation runs against the resolved shape; the value itself is discarded at type-check time.
pureType:
const config = {url: 'http://example.com', port: 8080};
return createValidate<typeof config>();
generated: function YOw_T3LhlXX(v){return (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && typeof v.url === 'string' && Number.isFinite(v.port))}
